title: Paul’s E-Portfolio
text:
Hello all! My name is Paul and I have worked in ecological restoration and resource management since completing my undergraduate in 2020 from the University of Victoria. Through working for a variety of non-profits, land trusts, and governmental organizations, I have become well versed in field work, geospatial data manipulation and analysis, visualizing results, and effectively communicating results to diverse stakeholders.

In my free time, I love to hike – bike – kayak – canoe or anything that gets me outside! On a rainy day, you will catch me reading a book, drinking ten different varieties of tea, and fixing an overdue DIY project.

Feel free to reach out via LinkedIn, GitHub , or email! Yours frondly,
Paul



Sir Donald Mountain, Glacier National Park

title: CV/Resume
text:
I am a terrestrial biologist with over five years of experience in the resource conservation sector, working across invasive plant management, terrestrial and aquatic restoration, and long-term small mammal monitoring programs. I hold a master’s degree and have extensive experience working with diverse spatial datasets, with strong proficiency in Python and R for data analysis and ecological modelling.
Since 2020, I have led native seed collection and high-elevation restoration initiatives, implemented integrated pest management strategies, and supported multi-taxa ecological integrity monitoring programs. I thrive in both field-based and office environments and enjoy collaborative work environments where constructive feedback and continuous learning are valued.

text:

Modelling critical habitat for the Eastern Whip-poor-will (Antrostomus vociferus) in Georgian Bay, Ontario: Comparing the maximum entropy model and multi-criteria decision analysis framework.

title: CV/Resume
text:
Resource Management Technician II
Mount Revelstoke and Glacier National Parks
May 2022 – Aug 2025

Planned and led implementation of the invasive plant management strategy for four field seasons
Assisted in Whitebark pine monitoring including:

Seedling assessments
Regional health transects
Cone caging and tree climbing
Seedling planting
Habitat suitability analysis

Supported ecological integrity monitoring programs including:

Pika monitoring
Amphibian monitoring
Bat acoustic monitoring

Completed remote camera classification and contributed to annual reporting

title: CV/Resume
section: Fire and Vegetation Student Technician / Crew Lead
text:
Fire and Vegetation Student Technician / Crew Lead
Banff National Park
Jun 2020 – Nov 2021

Collected, organized, and visualized geospatial data for long-term vegetation and invasive species management
Conducted riverine habitat assessments and whirling disease monitoring
Deployed and maintained water temperature loggers
Conducted CABIN monitoring using:

HACH velocity meters
YSI multi-parameter sondes

Completed the second year of eDNA trials in Johnson Lake

title: CV/Resume
text:
UVic Ecological Restoration Club (ERC) — Co-founder
May 2016 – Jun 2019

Co-founded the club and developed a five-year restoration plan for a degraded Camas meadow on the University of Victoria campus
Led programs to:

Seed native plants
Propagate native species
Transplant plants for meadow restoration

Collaborated with restoration organizations including:

Galiano Island Conservancy
Saanich Native Plants

title: Masters of Geomatics for Environmental Management
Masters of Geomatics for Environmental Management
Professional Experience
text:
Since 2020, I have worked in the Resource Management Department of Parks Canada in three National Parks: Banff, Glacier, and Revelstoke. In the Resource Management Department I have worked within Freshwater Aquatics, Fire and Vegetation, Wildlife Coexistence, and Ecological Integrity Monitoring teams. Within all of these roles I was responsible for the collection, maintenance, and analysis of geospatial data concerning a variety of habitats and projects.
Outside of resource conservation, I have experience working as a receptionist, cashier, barista, social media director, and fundraiser.

Professional Experience
text:
Since 2020, I have worked in the Resource Management Department of Parks Canada in three National Parks: Banff, Glacier, and Revelstoke. In the Resource Management Department I have worked within Freshwater Aquatics, Fire and Vegetation, Wildlife Coexistence, and Ecological Integrity Monitoring teams. Within all of these roles I was responsible for the collection, maintenance, and analysis of geospatial data concerning a variety of habitats and projects.
Outside of resource conservation, I have experience working as a receptionist, cashier, barista, social media director, and fundraiser.

title: Professional Experience
section: Mount Revelstoke and Glacier National Parks
text:
Mount Revelstoke and Glacier National Parks

2022 - present
From the summer of 2022 to the summer of 2025, I worked as a Resource Management Technician II for Mount Revelstoke and Glacier National Parks. Though some days were long and arduous, they were also some of most memorable in my career.
In this role:

Coordinated park-specific invasive plant management strategies
Supervised and taught field staff on plant identification, management strategies, and tree climbing techniques
Created subalpine restoration and native seed collection programs, and coordinated low elevation restoration programs
Assisted in long-term health studies of Endangered white bark pine ecosystems, collected and cleaned cones using arborist techniques, and co-created a 10-year restoration plan
Led the geospatial analysis of suitable white bark pine habitat to coordinate future restoration plans using VRI, ground truth data, NDVI and other vegetation metrics.
Assisted in winter tracking, remote camera deployment and image classification, and annual reporting.
